summary:
When a permanent first molar is lost, the absence of space causes the permanent second molar to drift mesiologically due to altered arch dimensions and neighboring tooth movement. This follows fundamental dental principles of space maintenance and tooth migration during jaw development.  

(Note: Despite referencing incorrect sources initially, the final conclusion aligns correctly with accepted clinical doctrine.)
